intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1. Run a standard Seurat workflow in R: load, QC, normalize, PCA, neighbors, clustering, UMAP, and FindAllMarkers; show me a UMAP and the top markers per cluster.

blocks.text: Good — I see three 10x triplet matrices (three samples total). You asked for the first one: **GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1**. Now let me load the Seurat workflow recipe to plan the analysis:

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "seurat-scrna", "description": "Authoritative single-cell RNA-seq QC + clustering + cluster annotation with R/Seurat (v5).", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + clustering + annotation with R/Seurat v5 (standard PBMC3k workflow). Provision: ensure_capability('Seurat'), then library(Seurat). THE THREE CHOICES THAT DEFINE THE RESULT \u2014 surface them with present_plan before committing: (1) QC thresholds (subset cutoffs on nFeature_RNA and percent.mt); (2) number of PCs/dims (read off the ElbowPlot); (3) clustering resolution (FindClusters). Steps: ReadMtx -> CreateSeuratObject(min.cells=3,min.features=200) -> PercentageFeatureSet('^MT-') -> subset QC -> NormalizeData -> FindVariableFeatures -> ScaleData -> RunPCA -> FindNeighbors -> FindClusters -> RunUMAP -> JoinLayers -> FindAllMarkers. Load ggplot2 and dplyr explicitly; never library(tidyverse)."}

blocks.text: Perfect. Now I'll present a plan before we begin:

input.title: Seurat QC → Clustering → Markers (GSM5746268)
input.summary: Load the 10x matrix triplet, run standard QC, normalization, PCA, clustering, and UMAP; identify and display top cluster markers.

steps.title: Load the 10x matrix triplet
steps.description: Read barcodes, features, and matrix.mtx files for GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1; create a Seurat object with min.cells=3, min.features=200.

steps.title: Mitochondrial QC
steps.description: Calculate percent mitochondrial content (MT- prefix) and add to metadata.

steps.title: QC-based subsetting
steps.description: Filter cells by nFeature_RNA and percent.mt. Standard thresholds: nFeature_RNA > 200, percent.mt < 10.

steps.title: Normalize, HVG, Scale
expected_outputs: Object with normalized and scaled assay

steps.title: PCA and nearest neighbors
expected_outputs: PCA reduction and nearest neighbors graph

steps.title: FindAllMarkers and visualization
steps.description: Identify marker genes per cluster; plot UMAP colored by cluster and generate a heatmap/table of top markers (e.g. top 3–5 per cluster).

assumptions: The data is mouse PBMC (Mus musculus) based on the accession prefix.
Mitochondrial genes use the MT- prefix (standard for mouse).
QC thresholds: nFeature_RNA > 200, percent.mt < 10 (standard conservative thresholds; can be adjusted post-QC if summary suggests wider bounds).
Clustering resolution = 0.8 (Seurat default; sensible for PBMC-like heterogeneity).
We'll use 30 PCs (standard for single-cell; can be adjusted from elbow plot if needed).
